description: People join timbers into a home, stones into a wall, or parts into a working system. 'lorima' builds a larger structure by assembling components or layers. 'kire' (shape) changes a part's configuration, while 'theko' (craft) says that practiced skill guides the making.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l' and let air pass along its sides into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Tap the tongue tip against the ridge before stressed high-front 'i', then close the lips for voiced 'm' and release into open central 'a'.
sound_symbolism: Lateral, tap, and lip closure place three distinct contacts in sequence. 'lorima' gathers itself by stages, a useful echo of assembly.
usage_notes: The structure being built is the direct object. Components, materials, tools, location, and collaborators take their ordinary phrases. Building can proceed horizontally or within an abstract system; the verb does not require upward construction.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Every building commits material and energy, then leaves questions of access and maintenance to the people who inherit it. 'lorima' names the construction clearly enough for those later questions to be asked.

semantic_domains.activity: assembling components or layers into a larger structure
semantic_domains.creation: constructing buildings, devices, or organised systems
semantic_domains.physical: joining material parts so that a structure stands or works
